chapter.title: The life of john the ELDER

paragraphs.text: AND THE CAVE OF SAPSAS to which Elijah the Tishbite was sent during a drought; it faces the Jordan.

There was an elder living in the monastery of Abba Eustorgios* whom our saintly Archbishop of Jerusalem wanted to appoint 2. THE ELDER WHO FED LIONS higoumen of the monastery. <The candidate> however wouid not IN HIS OWN CAVE agree and said: ‘I prefer prayer on Mount Sinai’. The archbishop® urged him first to become <higoumen> and then to depart <for the There was another elder at that place called Sapsas whose virtue mountain> but the elder would not be persuaded. THE LIFE OF CONON, PRIEST OF THE disciple® with him. They crossed the river Jordan® but before they COMMUNITY OF PENTHOUCLA reached even the first mile-post the elder began to shiver with lever.

So I said to him: nothing about it". Saint John then made him sit down on one of the ‘Now you are doing well, brother, and looking after your own soul’. hills, stripped him of his clothes and three times made the sign of He said to me: ‘Abba, I am about to die sir’,—and three days later the cross beneath his navel. ‘Believe me, Conon the priest’, he said, he was dead.

‘I wanted you to carry away some reward from the struggle. But This same Polychronios, priest of the New Lavra, also told me since you did not wish it to be so, I have caused the struggle to this: Once whilst I was staying at the Lavra of the Towers one of cease. But you shall have no reward for this’. Conon the priest the brothers died. The steward said to me: ‘Of your charity, brother, returned to the task of baptising at the community and next day he come so we can carry that brother’s effects into the storeroom’. As baptised and anointed the Persian withoul even being aware that she we began to move his things I saw the steward weeping. I said to was of the female sex. For twelve years he anointed and baptised him: "Come now, abba, why are you weeping in this way, sir’? He without suffering any physica) disturbance and with no awareness replied: ‘Because today I am carrying out that brother's effects and of women’s femininity; so he drew his life to a close. two days from now others shall bear away mine’. And so it was; two days later the steward himself died, just as he said.
